  • Uses of Command in io.github.projectunified.craftcommand.example.standalone

    Classes in io.github.projectunified.craftcommand.example.standalone with annotations of type Command
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    CalculatorCommand
    Comprehensive example command demonstrating all CraftCommand features.
    class 
    CalculatorCommand.AdvancedCommands
    Nested subcommand class demonstrating class-level subcommands.
    Methods in io.github.projectunified.craftcommand.example.standalone with annotations of type Command
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    CalculatorCommand.add(Object sender, int num1, int num2)
     
    void
    CalculatorCommand.divide(Object sender, int num1, int num2)
     
    void
    CalculatorCommand.echo(Object sender, String message)
    Demonstrates @Greedy on String (joins remaining args with spaces).
    void
    CalculatorCommand.enumOp(Object sender, CalculatorCommand.MathOp op, int num1, int num2)
    Demonstrates enum parameter resolution (via registerProvider in CLIApp).
    void
    CalculatorCommand.AdvancedCommands.logarithm(Object sender, double value, double base)
    Demonstrates nested class with its own @Default parameters.
    void
    CalculatorCommand.multiply(Object sender, int num1, int num2)
     
    void
    CalculatorCommand.parse(Object sender, double value)
    Demonstrates @Greedy on non-String type (joins remaining args, then parses).
    void
    CalculatorCommand.AdvancedCommands.power(Object sender, double base, double exponent)
     
    void
    CalculatorCommand.print(Object sender, String prefix, String text)
    Demonstrates @Default with default value on String.
    void
    CalculatorCommand.repeat(Object sender, String text, int count)
    Demonstrates @Default with default value on int.
    void
    CalculatorCommand.runMode(Object sender, String mode)
    Demonstrates method-based @Suggest with sender, args, current parameters.
    void
    CalculatorCommand.runOp(Object sender, String op, int num1, int num2)
    Demonstrates field-based @Suggest for operator selection.
    void
    CalculatorCommand.runPoint(Object sender, CalculatorCommand.Point pt)
    Demonstrates parameter-level @Resolve with named method.
    void
    CalculatorCommand.sendMessage(Object sender, String target, String message)
    Demonstrates @Name to override parameter name in usage messages.
    void
    CalculatorCommand.setCoordinate(Object sender, double x, double y, double z)
    Demonstrates @ValidateWith with custom validation method.
    void
    CalculatorCommand.setLevel(Object sender, int level, double multiplier)
    Demonstrates @Min and @Max validation on numeric types.
    void
    CalculatorCommand.AdvancedCommands.sqrt(Object sender, double number)
     
    void
    CalculatorCommand.subtract(Object sender, int num1, int num2)
     
    void
    CalculatorCommand.sum(Object sender, int[] numbers)
    Demonstrates @Greedy on array type (creates array from remaining args).
    void
    CalculatorCommand.whoAmI(CalculatorCommand.CustomSender sender)
    Demonstrates sender-level @Resolve.
